properties.remark: ESTIMATED TIME BASED ON STORM LOCATION.
OUTFLOW FROM STORM NEAR MT ROSE. ALSO PRODUCED WIND GUSTS AROUND 45 MPH IN NORTHERN WASHOE VALLEY AND PLEASANT VALLEY AREAS OF HIGHWAY 395.
OUTFLOW FROM STORM NEAR MT ROSE. ALSO PRODUCED WIND GUSTS AROUND 45 MPH IN NORTHERN WASHOE VALLEY AND PLEASANT VALLEY AREAS OF HIGHWAY 395.
COTTONWOOD TREE BRANCHES UP TO 2 INCH DIAMETER BLOWN DOWN AROUND GOVERNORS MANSION. DOWNED BRANCHES ALSO KNOCKED OUT POWERLINES IN CARSON CITY. OUTFLOW WIND GUSTS.
COTTONWOOD TREE BRANCHES UP TO 1.5 INCH DIAMETER BLOWN DOWN AROUND PARTS OF YERINGTON. ESTIMATED OCCURRENCE BETWEEN 4 AND 5 PM.

REPORT OF A FLASH FLOOD COVERING MILAN DRIVE NEAR USA PARKWAY. UNKNOWN IF THE ROAD WAS WASHED AWAY, BUT A SIGNIFICANT AMOUNT OF WATER AND DEBRIS WERE COVERING ROADWAY. SEVERAL PICTURES SUBMITTED BY NEWS MEDIA AND PUBLIC.

PICTURE SENT IN OF A SECTION OF HIGHWAY 447 WASHED OUT WITH TWO FEET OF WATER FLOWING OVER IT. AT ONE POINT IT WAS REPORTED TO BE SIX FEET DEEP. ANOTHER SECTION OF THE HIGHWAY WAS REPORTEDLY WASHED OUT FURTHER NORTH. TIME UPDATED PER NDOT.
FLOODING OVER ROADWAY REPORTED ON STATE ROUTE 447 FIVE MILES NORTH OF GERLACH. CLOSED DOWN ROUTE 447.
WASHOE COUNTY ROAD CREWS REPORTING ROAD FLOODING FROM LA POSADA TO CALLE DA LA PLATA EAST OF PYRAMID HIGHWAY.

PICTURE SENT IN OF A SECTION OF HIGHWAY 447 WASHED OUT WITH TWO FEET OF WATER FLOWING OVER IT. AT ONE POINT IT WAS REPORTED TO BE SIX FEET DEEP. ANOTHER SECTION OF THE HIGHWAY WAS REPORTEDLY WASHED OUT FURTHER NORTH.
NDOT SENSOR REPORTED WIND GUSTS 45-60 MPH BETWEEN 2 PM AND 5 PM.
JUNIPER SPRINGS RAWS
60 MPH WIND GUST.
HEAVY RAIN WITH 1 INCH HAIL REPORTED IN GOLDEN VALLEY.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF 1 INCH HAIL IN THE GOLDEN VALLEY AREA.
RECEIVED PHOTO OF HAILSTONES NEAR STEAD AIRPORT BETWEEN 1 AND 1 1/2 INCH DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED NEAR 630 PM.
64 MPH GUST OBSERVED AT RENO NWS OFFICE.
MUDSLIDE OVER INTERSTATE 80 NEAR THE CALIFORNIA NEVADA STATE LINE. CALTRANS IS REPORTING ON THEIR WEBSITE THAT INTERSTATE 80 IS CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
ASOS OBSERVATION AT FALLON NAVAL AIR STATION REPORTED 1/8 MILE VISIBILITY, WIND GUST TO 60 MPH IN A DUST STORM.
VISIBILITY LESS THAN 1/2 MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
OUTFLOW WIND GUSTS ON ANAHO ISLAND. SEVERAL GUSTS UP TO 58 MPH BETWEEN 530 AND 7 PM.
REPORTED BY THE MONO COUNTY SHERIFF
MONO COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTING WATER IN HOMES ALONG HIGHWAY 182 IN BRIDEPORT. EXTENT OF DAMAGE IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.
MONO COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTING WATER IN HOMES ALONG HIGHWAY 182 IN BRIDEPORT. 8 HOMES RECEIVED WATER DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD FLOODED BASEMENT AND A COUPLE OF INCHES OF WATER ON THE MAIN FLOOR.
ALSO RECEIVED 0.95 INCH OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
TRAINED STORM SPOTTER REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL AND VERY HEAVY RAINFALL.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED HEAVY RAIN AND 1 INCH HAIL.
LAW ENFORECEMENT REPORTED FLOODING ON MAIN ST. OVER THE RAILROAD TRACKS, 10 FT. SINK HOLE ON SEA BISCUIT DRIVE AND CABLE CANYON RD AND 3 FEET OF STANDING WATER 300 YARDS LONG ON SCOTT DRIVE.
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED ON THE NORTHERN SIDE OF MASON VALLEY AND RELAYED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT.
NDOT REPORTING I-80 WESTBOUND IS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
A PARK RANGER REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL AT THE LAHONTAN RESERVOIR.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED MUD, DEBRIS AND SMALL ROCKS COMING DOWN FROM THE MOUNTAIN DRAINAGES JUST WEST OF WEED HEIGHTS.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTING A SEVERE MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS MCLEOD HILL NEAR HWY95A AND GOLDFIELD AVE.

THIRD VERY HEAVY RAIN EVENT TODAY IN FERNLEY. OVER ONE INCH TOTAL PRECIPITATION. FLOODING OBSERVED IN FERNLEY AREA WITHIN THE HOUR INCLUDED WATER IN SEVERAL GARAGES IN PORTIONS OF TOWN AND FLOODING IN CENTRAL FERNLEY AT WENDYS RESTAURANT.
LYON COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTING HOUSES AND YARDS FLOODING ALONG SADDLE HORN, MALLARD WAY, JENNYS LANE. ALSO A REPORT OF A RETENTION POND FLOODING ON COLDWATER DR.
REPORTED AT MAJUBA RAWS SITE. PROBABLE TIME OF PEAK GUST BETWEEN 9 AND 930 PM AS THHUNDERSTORM MOVED OVERHEAD. ALSO REPORTED 0.58 INCH OF RAIN IN LESS THAN 1 HOUR.

TRAINED SPOTTER IN HOMEWOOD OBSERVED HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
SIX TO EIGHT INCHES OF MUD AND DEBRIS COVERING WEST BOUND INTERSTATE 80 NEAR THE CALIFORNIA-NEVADA STATELINE. INTERSTATE 80 WEST BOUND IS CLOSED UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED WATER AND DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS THE ROADWAY AT RED ROCK ROAD AND ARGOSY ROAD.
REPORT OF DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY 338 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 6 AND 8 WITH TRAFFIC CONTROLS IN PLACE. NO INDICATION OF ROAD CLOSURE.
HIGHWAY 338 AT MILE MARKER 338 WAS CLOSED AT 702 PM AS A RESULT OF PREVIOUS HEAVY RAIN AND DEBRIS ON THE HIGHWAY. NDOT IS WORKING TO REOPEN AT THIS TIME.
NDOT HAS REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AND A ROAD WASH OUT ON NEVADA STATE ROUTE 338 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 9 AND 12.
58 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED BY THE SURPRISE VALLEY RAWS.
AT LOVELOCK AIRPORT ASOS. BLOWING DUST ALSO OBSERVED WITH VISIBILITY DROPPING TO 4 MILES.
NEVADA DOT SENSOR AT TOULON ALONG INTERSTATE 80. TIME APPROXIMATE.
3 1/2 TO 4 MILE EAST OF FERNLEY, TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THUNDERSTORM OVERHEAD, NO RAIN. BLOWING DUST TO SOUTH WITH 1 MILE VISIBILITY.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 1/4 MILE IN BLOWING DUST AND SAND.
OUTFLOW GUST REPORTED AT NDOT WIND SENSOR.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THUNDERSTORM, LIGHT RAIN, WINDS ESTIMATED AT 45 MPH AND 1/4 MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
30 FEET OF WOOD FENCE AND 3 INCH DIAMETER APPLE TREE BLOWN OVER. ESTIMATED GUST 50 MPH.
MEASURED AT THE JUNIPER SPRINGS RAWS. GUST TIME ESTIMATED.
